page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware accelerators.

Benchmark results reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance in the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 25%. However, the server leverages its 64-core architecture to deliver strong multi-threaded performance. PassMark CPU scores, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests, rank in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also performs in the top 25%, while Redis SET operations reach a strong 62.83 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of this server lies in its shared CPU allocation and low single-core clock speed of 2.0 GHz, which limits its suitability for single-threaded tasks. However, for highly parallelized workloads, the resource density of 64 physical cores combined with 256.0 GB of RAM offers a cost-efficient profile. It delivers average to strong multi-core performance without the premium cost associated with dedicated, high-frequency compute instances.

This configuration is best suited for multi-threaded, parallel workloads that can distribute tasks across its 64 cores. Appropriate use cases include software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, Redis-based caching, and general-purpose web serving. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it offers a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. While single-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng and single-threaded compression, rank in the lower percentiles, the server excels in multi-threaded environments. It achieves strong performance in PassMark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations, which reach 62.83 million operations per second. This shared-CPU instance provides a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized workloads, making it well-suited for software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, caching, and general-purpose web serving where single-core speed is not the primary bottleneck.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-threaded performance and high resource density for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for parallel multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core virtual server featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and balanced memory for highly parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Sixty-four AMD EPYC 7542 cores with 256 GB of system memory
Strong multi-threaded performance in PassMark and decompression benchmarks
High-throughput Redis performance reaching 62.83 million operations per second
Shared CPU allocation offering cost-efficient parallel processing
Best suited for software compilation, caching, and multi-threaded workloads
